key_insight: Random sampling maps provide a unified way to compare and sketch inputs of different sizes, enabling theory for generalization from small to large instances in variable-size learning problems.
engineering_relevance: This gives practical guidance for designing and evaluating scalable models on sequences, graphs, point clouds, and tensors when training and inference sizes differ.
method: Define size-bridging sampling maps such as sampling with replacement, random binning, and species sampling.
Match each sampling scheme to the symmetries and cross-size relations of the data domain.
Derive explicit generalization and sketching rates for sampling-continuous function classes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical characterization and rate results across families including moment polynomials, graph homomorphism densities, permutation-invariant transformers, and graph neural networks, rather than a new empirical benchmark.
watch_item: The framework depends on choosing the right sampling notion for the domain, so mismatched symmetries or relations could weaken the guarantees in practice.
